2019-09-26.abstract: The German authorities suspect the well-planned killing of a former Chechen separatist in Berlin was carried out by a Russian hit man released from prison for that purpose.

2019-09-26.title: A Desperate Battle, and a Victory for Now, at a Remote Afghan Outpost
2019-09-26.abstract: Struggling Afghan forces took three northern districts from the Taliban. But the cost was high, and troops depend on American airstrikes to hold on.

2019-09-26.title: Women in Parliament Call Out Boris Johnson for His Incendiary Language on Brexit
2019-09-26.abstract: The prime minister this week came up with new slurs for those who disagree with him about Britain’s departure from the European Union, saying they were humiliating their country.

2019-09-26.title: Barred From Owning Land, Rural Chinese Miss Spoils of Country’s Success
2019-09-26.abstract: A region that is the cradle of China’s economic overhauls shows how decisions made four decades ago are hobbling China’s farmers and hampering economic growth.

2019-09-27.title: Are the Kids Alright in the Era of Climate Change?
2019-09-27.abstract: The dissonance between slow action by politicians and increasing urgency from scientists has made young people more angry than anxious.

2019-09-27.title: British-Flagged Tanker Leaves Iran, Two Months After It Was Seized
2019-09-27.abstract: The Stena Impero, which Iranian authorities impounded in July, was heading to Dubai, where crew members will undergo medical checks.

2019-09-27.title: Afghanistan Presidential Vote Presses On Despite Concerns About Legitimacy
2019-09-27.abstract: Accusations of systemic fraud and fears of Taliban threats have compromised an election that is likely to have the fewest voters since the first presidential vote in 2004.

2019-09-27.title: In Hong Kong, Unity Between Peaceful and Radical Protesters. For Now.
2019-09-27.abstract: Five years ago, protesters were critical of even breaking windows. Now, some are throwing firebombs.

2019-09-27.title: Saudi Arabia Plans to Let More Foreign Tourists Have Visas
2019-09-27.abstract: The notoriously repressive kingdom will announce a new program as it tries to diversify its economy away from dependence on oil.

2019-09-28.title: Afghanistan Election Draws Low Turnout Amid Taliban Threats
2019-09-28.abstract: With peace talks on hold, the Taliban have stepped up attacks, and Afghans fear that a bitterly contested presidential election could lead to political paralysis.

2019-09-28.title: Austria’s Kurz, Leading in Polls, Looks Open to Reunion With Far Right
2019-09-28.abstract: Four months after scandal imploded Sebastian Kurz’s coalition, the wunderkind conservative looks set to win again in Sunday’s election. Will he embrace the far right a second time?

2019-09-28.title: A Father-Son Split on Hong Kong Protests Shows City’s Generational Divide
2019-09-28.abstract: As China celebrates the 70th anniversary of Communist rule, a father who fled the country wonders if he made the right decision, and why his son is protesting.

2019-09-28.title: A Chim Chiminey Parade Honors Sweeps and Recalls Past Horrors
2019-09-28.abstract: The Italian town said to be the cradle of chimney sweeps was once ashamed of its ties to the trade. Now, a festival draws sweeps from around the world, who celebrate without forgetting past miseries.

2019-09-28.title: Dutch Railroad Reckons With Holocaust Shame, 70 Years Later
2019-09-28.abstract: Even as the national railroad company in the Netherlands begins paying compensation to the relatives of those it transported to death camps, new details of its collaboration are emerging.

2019-09-29.title: Soweto, Once Unified Against Apartheid, Is Now Divided by Wealth
2019-09-29.abstract: The township was once a symbol of united resistance to a racist regime in South Africa. Now it embodies the class divisions in the country’s black majority.

2019-09-29.title: Major Clashes Erupt in Hong Kong Ahead of China’s National Day
2019-09-29.abstract: The demonstrations openly defy Beijing’s rule just two days before the central government celebrates 70 years of Communist rule.

2019-09-29.title: U.S. Businesswoman Admitted Affair With Boris Johnson, U.K. Report Says
2019-09-29.abstract: The woman, Jennifer Arcuri, reportedly told friends that she was having an affair with Mr. Johnson when he was mayor, deepening the scandal as he tries to navigate an impasse over Brexit.

2019-09-29.title: Unveiling Monument to Migrants, Pope Francis Urges Compassion for Refugees
2019-09-29.abstract: The pope unveiled the sculpture in St. Peter’s Square during a special Mass and portrayed migration as an opportunity to “promote the integral human development of all people.”

2019-09-29.title: Luxury Cars Seized From African Leader’s Son Are Auctioned in Geneva
2019-09-29.abstract: Vehicles linked to an inquiry into graft in Equatorial Guinea yielded $27 million at auction.

2019-09-30.title: U.S. Sees Rising Threat in the West From Qaeda Branch in Syria
2019-09-30.abstract: American counterterrorism officials are voicing alarm about a Qaeda affiliate in Syria that they say is plotting attacks from the country’s northwest.

2019-09-30.title: Impeachment, Boris Johnson, Austria Elections: Your Monday Briefing
2019-09-30.abstract: Here’s what you need to know

2019-09-30.title: Gems, Warlords and Mercenaries: Russia’s Playbook in Central African Republic
2019-09-30.abstract: After meddling in the 2016 American election, Russia is using similar tactics in the Central African Republic. But as it sows political chaos, this time it is also seeking diamonds.

2019-09-30.title: Xi Extols China’s ‘Red’ Heritage in a Land Haunted by Famine Under Mao
2019-09-30.abstract: Seventy years after Mao founded the People’s Republic, Xi Jinping visited Xinyang to rekindle revolutionary traditions. Its survivors of mass starvation went unmentioned.

2019-09-30.title: Parades and Protests on China’s 70th Anniversary
2019-09-30.abstract: As the Communist Party celebrates 70 years in power, protests in Hong Kong threaten to overshadow the festivities.
